title: Fixing ~/.ssh/identity handling
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes

I have spotted an issue when accessing remote podman host if `~/.ssh/identity` file does not exist.

```text
 % podman version                                                                                                                                       
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.3.1
API Version:  5.3.1
Go Version:   go1.23.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.3.1
API Version:  5.3.1
Go Version:   go1.22.7
Built:        Thu Nov 21 00:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

Please note that there is no `Identity` for monster connection (I am relying on ssh-agent)
```text
% podman system connection add monster ssh://monster/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock

% podman system connection list                                                                                                                        
Name                         URI                                                         Identity                                                      Default     ReadWrite
monster                      ssh://monster:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock                                                                         false       true
podman-machine-default       ssh://core@127.0.0.1:63138/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock  /Users/valera/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/machine  false       true
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@127.0.0.1:63138/run/podman/podman.sock           /Users/valera/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/machine  true        true
```

Without patch I am getting the following result
```text
% podman --debug -c monster ps -a                                                                                                                      
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --debug -c monster ps -a)
DEBU[0000] ssh_config alias found: monster
DEBU[0000]   User: valera
DEBU[0000]   Hostname: ssh://monster:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
DEBU[0000]   Port: 22
DEBU[0000]   IdentityFile: "/Users/valera/.ssh/identity"
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to read identity "/Users/valera/.ssh/identity": open /Users/valera/.ssh/identity: no such file or directory
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines
 

 % stat ~/.ssh/identity                                                                                                                                 
stat: /Users/valera/.ssh/identity: stat: No such file or directory
```
After fix I am able to connect successfully
```text
%  ./bin/darwin/podman --debug -c monster ps -a                                                                                                        DAN/podman (fixing-ssh-identity) mbp16-pvt
INFO[0000] ./bin/darwin/podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(./bin/darwin/podman --debug -c monster ps -a)
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/Users/valera/.local/share/wezterm/agent.1868", ssh-agent signer enabled
DEBU[0000] SSH Agent Key SHA256:137xVFb8sE80Yz0+CiEO0KV5ic7unpNo2qxGXO+TzVc ssh-ed25519
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.4.0/libpod/_ping
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v5.4.0/libpod/containers/json
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPostRunE(./bin/darwin/podman --debug -c monster ps -a)
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines

```

Please let me know if you are happy or more changes required

```release-note
Fixing ~/.ssh/identity handling
```

comments.body: need rebase to one commit and add test
Code LGTM. Is this something we can reasonably test?

> Code LGTM. Is this something we can reasonably test?

No, not with the current state of the code (correct me if I am wrong), but I can factor  the check out into a separate function and add a couple of tests for it (create a `connection_test.go).
Let me know your opinion, there might be other concerns I am not aware of.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *vmasutin* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
